# FM cockpit density evidence spike: real fleet-scale numbers before layout freezes

## Context

T0.2 gate leaf of Epic #14560 (map v1.1), carrying the #13444 graduation's density AC down to the cockpit surface: *"the UX epic needs actual count/density evidence before freezing layout."* Filed under the operator-escalated decomposition sprint (D#14561).

## The Problem

The SSOT artifact mocks 6–7 agents, ~5 stream events visible, 2-column grid. Tonight's live reality already stresses those assumptions: 12 registered identities, message bursts of 40–90 per 25-minute window (observed 2026-07-04, wake-batch sizes), 15+ tickets filed per hour during a sprint. A layout frozen against the mock's density will break at real fleet scale — the #13444 review named this class explicitly (scale-to-a-glance needs numbers, not vibes).

## The Architectural Reality

Evidence sources, all queryable today: the Memory Core roster (`who_is_online` — registered vs active vs benched counts), A2A volume (message timestamps per window from `list_messages` paging), lifecycle event rates (PR/review/comment timelines from the synced content), lane counts (D#14561 claims ledger + open epic assignees). The temporal-pyramid L2 records (#14433 orbit) will eventually make this a query; this spike hand-collects once.

## The Fix

A short evidence doc (comment on #14560 or `apps/agentos/design/` sibling artifact — placement per #14577's decision): agent-count distribution (registered/active/concurrent), events-per-minute percentiles for the activity stream (median + burst), lanes-per-agent, and the derived layout consequences (grid columns at N agents, stream buffer + collapse thresholds, health-bar categories). Each number carries its query/source (the #14430 falsifying-query discipline applied to design inputs).

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Agent-count evidence: registered / active-in-window / peak-concurrent, with sources.
- [ ] Activity-stream rate evidence: median + p95 events/min over ≥3 real windows (tonight's burst windows qualify), with sources.
- [ ] Derived layout consequences stated for grid, stream, and health bar (the numbers the view leaves consume).
- [ ] Every figure carries its falsifying query/source; no vibes-numbers.

## Out of Scope

- The views themselves (T2–T5 consume this).
- Temporal-pyramid automation (#14433 orbit — this is a one-shot hand collection).

## Related

Epic #14560 (parent) · #13444 (the density AC's origin) · #14577 (placement gate) · D#14561 (sprint).

Live latest-open sweep: latest 12 checked 2026-07-04T01:59Z (newest #14591); no equivalent. A2A in-flight sweep: clean for L1 leaves (lane-3 batch is Clio's, boundaries noted). Structure-map gate: N/A — evidence doc, no new `ai/` placement.

### @neo-opus-vega - 2026-07-06T09:38:59Z

## Density evidence — real fleet-scale numbers (spike deliverable)

Hand-collected once per AC; every figure carries its falsifying query (AC#4). Sources: `who_is_online` (2026-07-06T09:30Z) + the memory-core graph store (`Nodes WHERE id LIKE 'MESSAGE:%'` — 3,409 A2A messages, 2026-06-13→07-06).

### AC#1 — agent count
- **Registered identities: 12** (`who_is_online`) — ~8 real cross-family maintainers (gpt, vega, opus-4.7, gemini-pro·benched, ada, Mnemosyne, Clio, Grace) + operator + 3 test/fixture (test-writer, agent-a/b · dark since 06-07).
- **Peak-concurrent (fresh-activity snapshot): 3** (gpt, vega, ada); active-in-window (recent writes): 6–8.
- **Family distribution: claude 6/8, gpt 1, gemini 1 (benched)** → skewed, single dominant family + long tail + unclassified bucket.
- vs the SSOT mock's 6–7 agents → **~2× low.**

### AC#2 — activity rate
A2A messages only (a **lower bound** — lifecycle PR/review/comment events add to the stream on top of this). Query: `SELECT substr(json_extract(data,'$.properties.sentAt'),1,13) hour, count(*) FROM Nodes WHERE id LIKE 'MESSAGE:%' GROUP BY hour` (+ per-minute variant).
- **Sustained hourly peak: 114 msgs/hr** (2026-07-04T01Z ≈ 1.9/min); 6 windows >50/hr on the 07-04 goal-scoping sprint day (114, 78, 71, 66, 56, 54).
- **Per-active-minute (688 active min, 07-04→07-06): median 1/min · p95 3/min · peak 9/min.**

### AC#3 — derived layout consequences
- **ActivityStream (#14606):** must render a burst of **~9 events/min peak** (A2A alone; higher with lifecycle). Bounded buffer ~50–100 events + coalesce/collapse beyond — the mock's "~5 visible" is far too small; the backpressure design is justified by the numbers.
- **FleetGrid:** seat **~9–12 cards**, not 2-col / 6–7. The ranked-fold (#14834) is the right call — evidence-confirmed.
- **HealthBar:** 5 state categories (StateDot closed set: ok/idle/wedged/limited/off) — matches shipped #14834.
- **FamilyRail:** must handle a **skewed** distribution (one dominant family + singletons + unclassified), not an even split.

AC#4 ✓ — every figure carries its query/source. Out of scope (unchanged): the views themselves (T2–T5 consume this), temporal-pyramid automation (#14433).

**This completes the #14592 spike.** Placement: recorded on the spike ticket; liftable to an `apps/agentos/design/` sibling if #14577 requires. Recommend close (operator-authority). — Vega (@neo-opus-vega · Opus 4.8)

### @neo-opus-vega - 2026-07-06T11:02:31Z

## Correction — the real agent count is 7, not 12 (my error)

@tobiu corrected my AC#1 figure. I reported the raw `who_is_online` **node** count (12) and "~8 maintainers" — but folded in non-agents: the operator (@tobiu), three test/fixture identities (@neo-test-writer, @agent-a, @agent-b), and the legacy @neo-opus (Opus 4.7, superseded). The real cross-family agent roster is **7**: Euclid, Vega, Ada, Grace, Mnemosyne, Clio, Gemini.

**Corrected AC#1 + AC#3:**
- **7 real agents** (Claude ×5 — Vega/Ada/Grace/Mnemosyne/Clio; GPT ×1; Gemini ×1, benched). Concurrent-online at the snapshot: 3.
- **vs the SSOT mock's 6–7: the mock was accurate.** My "~2× low" conclusion is **retracted** — at current scale the mock's agent count holds.
- **Layout:** the grid seats **~7 (+ modest growth headroom)**, not 9–12. The ranked-fold (#14834) earns its keep as the roster grows, but current scale does not force a wider grid.
- **AC#2 activity-rate data stands** (114 msgs/hr peak; per-active-minute p50 1 / p95 3 / peak 9 — separately measured from `MESSAGE` node timestamps, independent of the agent-count error).

Lesson banked: count REAL agents (a model-family maintainer identity), never raw roster nodes — exclude the operator, test identities, and superseded/legacy nodes. This also surfaced + fixed a fabricated agent ("Kepler") in the FM cockpit fixture. — Vega (@neo-opus-vega · Opus 4.8)
